CREATE TABLE intervals (id INT PRIMARY KEY, duration INTERVAL DAY TO SECOND);
INSERT INTO intervals VALUES (1, INTERVAL '10 12:30:45.123' DAY TO SECOND);
INSERT INTO intervals VALUES (2, INTERVAL '5 6:00:00' DAY TO SECOND);
INSERT INTO intervals VALUES (3, INTERVAL '0 1:15:30' DAY TO SECOND);
INSERT INTO intervals VALUES (4, INTERVAL '3 0:0:0.456' DAY TO SECOND);
SELECT id, duration FROM intervals WHERE duration > INTERVAL '2 0:0:0' DAY TO SECOND;
SELECT id, EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM duration) AS seconds FROM intervals WHERE EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM duration) < 600;
SELECT id, duration + INTERVAL '1 2:0:0.500' DAY TO SECOND AS new_duration FROM intervals;
SELECT id, duration - INTERVAL '0 0:30:00' DAY TO SECOND AS shortened_duration FROM intervals;
WITH temp AS (SELECT id, duration FROM intervals WHERE duration IS NOT NULL) SELECT COUNT(*) FROM temp;
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, start_time TIMESTAMP);
SELECT a.id, a.start_time, a.start_time + i.duration AS end_time FROM another_table a JOIN intervals i ON a.id = i.id;
SELECT id, duration FROM intervals WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM another_table WHERE start_time < '2023-10-03 00:00:00');
SELECT id, duration, duration * INTERVAL '2' DAY TO SECOND AS extended_duration FROM intervals;
SELECT id, duration / INTERVAL '1' DAY TO SECOND AS duration_in_days FROM intervals WHERE duration > INTERVAL '1 0:0:0' DAY TO SECOND;
